evidence: `crates/temper-server/src/api.rs:1-5` declares `/api` as mutation-focused and separate from read-only `/observe`, but routes in `api.rs:42-65` dispatch to `crate::observe::*` handlers for spec loading, wasm writes, and evolution decisions/checks.

`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution.rs:1,8-14` re-exports both read and write-oriented handlers (`handle_decide`, `handle_unmet_intent`, `handle_sentinel_check`) under the observe namespace, blurring read/write ownership.

`crates/temper-server/src/authz_helpers.rs:42-143` is packaged as a helper but performs orchestration across pending decisions, trajectory persistence, and governance entity dispatch, which is broader than helper-level responsibility.

`crates/temper-server/src/observe/agents.rs:85-112` and `crates/temper-server/src/observe/entities.rs:63-125` embed storage/actor-source selection and data access directly in route modules, so HTTP modules also own query composition and source fallback policy.

`crates/temper-observe/src/lib.rs:8-11` describes trait-centered provider abstraction, but `lib.rs:21,31` publicly exposes `clickhouse` and `ClickHouseStore`, coupling top-level API to one backend implementation.

IOA guard/effect translation is implemented twice with parallel logic in `crates/temper-jit/src/table/builder.rs` (guard/effect mapping and name-based fallback at lines 45-175) and `crates/temper-verify/src/model/builder.rs` (parallel mapping and fallback at lines 89-185).

Server OData helpers repeatedly bridge two storage abstractions (registry-first + legacy fallback) in `crates/temper-server/src/odata/common.rs` (`resolve_entity_type`, `tenant_csdl_xml`, `tenant_entity_sets` at lines 62-98), while other spec readers like `crates/temper-server/src/observe/specs.rs` use registry-only paths.

Authz boundary requires repeated map-shape translation from deterministic `BTreeMap` to `HashMap` in `crates/temper-server/src/state/entity_ops.rs` (`authorize` and `authorize_with_context`, lines 205-243 and 253-290) because `crates/temper-authz/src/engine.rs` only accepts `HashMap` inputs (lines 90-96, 223-224).

`crates/temper-cli/src/serve/mod.rs::run` (lines 60-260) orchestrates backend selection, recovery, tenant loading, webhook loading, event-store wiring, hydration, and policy reload in one flow, with boundary logic repeated for Postgres/Turso branches and minimal seam isolation.

Several assigned modules are single-purpose passthrough barrels with no local behavior (`crates/temper-cli/src/util.rs`, `crates/temper-mcp/src/repl.rs`, `crates/temper-jit/src/table/mod.rs`, `crates/temper-odata/src/query/mod.rs`), increasing hop count for basic symbol discovery.

A deprecated API is kept alive through crate root with a crate-level suppression (`#[allow(deprecated)] pub use store::RecordStore;` in `crates/temper-evolution/src/lib.rs:27`) while adjacent model types still carry migration-state fields (`migration_required` in `crates/temper-evolution/src/records.rs:232`).

Error enums in adjacent crates rely on mostly opaque `String` payload variants (`crates/temper-authz/src/error.rs`, `crates/temper-mcp/src/error.rs`, `crates/temper-observe/src/error.rs`), which weakens local precision and encourages message-shaped control flow.
low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Local implementations are generally compact, but repeated passthrough modules, deprecated-export bridging, and stringly error internals add avoidable navigation and maintenance friction.

evidence: History fallback path reads persistence IDs as `entity_type:entity_id` in `crates/temper-server/src/observe/entities.rs:101`, while actor persistence writes IDs as `tenant:entity_type:entity_id` in `crates/temper-server/src/entity_actor/actor.rs:105-107`.

Evolution persistence swallows JSON serialization failures with defaults (e.g. `unwrap_or_default`, `unwrap_or_else(|_| "[]")`) in `crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/operations.rs:63,89,116,223`, and listing endpoints silently skip malformed JSON merge in `crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:40-47,98-105`.

Tenant fallback behavior is inconsistent and startup-state dependent: API handlers force `"default"` (`crates/temper-server/src/api.rs:1070-1073,1130-1133,1195-1199`) while OData/observe fallback to first registered tenant then default (`crates/temper-server/src/odata/common.rs:23-36`).

Webhook secret/header interpolation is done once at load (`crates/temper-server/src/webhooks.rs:72-85,178-189`) and the dispatcher is built once during server startup (`crates/temper-cli/src/serve/mod.rs:194-218`), so runtime env changes or late secret injection are ignored; missing vars silently become empty strings.

findings.summary: One module owns multiple evolution domains and policy heuristics.
evidence: `insight_generator.rs` implements insight ranking, unmet-intent lifecycle inference, feature-request generation, dedup-key parsing, and error taxonomy in one file (lines 48-235, 241-311, 336-402, 318-329, 414-429).

The API layer in `records_list.rs` separately re-shapes persisted evolution records/insights (lines 30-52, 92-109), so evolution behavior is split between generation policy and response-shaping policy without a clear service boundary.
findings.suggestion: Split evolution into dedicated modules/services (for example `insights`, `unmet_intents`, `feature_requests`) behind a small `EvolutionQueryService`/`EvolutionAnalysisService` interface used by observe handlers; keep classification/error taxonomy in shared typed policy modules to avoid policy scattering.

findings.summary: Backend-specific mirror flows duplicate the same domain restore/upsert workflow.
evidence: `load_registry_from_postgres` and `load_registry_from_turso` repeat the same grouping, CSDL parse, tenant registration, and status restoration algorithm with backend-specific row retrieval (storage.rs lines 212-294 and 297-369).

`upsert_loaded_specs_to_postgres` and `upsert_loaded_specs_to_turso` duplicate tenant/spec upsert semantics in two codepaths (lines 77-128 and 473-499), increasing drift risk whenever persistence rules change.
`records_list.rs` reaches directly into Turso from handler methods (lines 24-27, 89-90), reinforcing backend-specific coupling at call sites instead of through a unified domain repository.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a backend-agnostic repository trait (or adapter layer) for spec restore/upsert/evolution queries and centralize the domain algorithm once; backend adapters should only implement row fetch/store primitives.

findings.summary: Dispatch ownership is obscured by passing a wide cross-domain request bag.
evidence: `BlockingWasmDispatch` aggregates tenant identity, entity identity, action, effects, state snapshot, agent context, and action params in one struct (dispatch_blocking.rs lines 13-22), then just forwards all fields to internal dispatch (lines 38-47).

The method returns a boxed future from an `impl ServerState` extension (lines 31-36), making the state root object a broad orchestration surface rather than exposing narrower collaborators for integration dispatch.
findings.suggestion: Extract a dedicated integration-dispatch service with a typed command object organized by cohesive sub-objects (identity, invocation, context); keep `ServerState` as composition root and delegate via a stable trait boundary.

findings.summary: Runtime and verifier duplicate IOA guard/effect translation logic
evidence: `TransitionTable::from_automaton` maps IOA guards/effects and applies name-based fallback heuristics (`additem`/`removeitem`) in `crates/temper-jit/src/table/builder.rs` lines 45-175.
`resolve_transitions` + `translate_guards` + `translate_effects` repeat equivalent translation decisions and the same name-based fallback in `crates/temper-verify/src/model/builder.rs` lines 89-185.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared IOA-to-execution translation layer (or a shared normalized IR) consumed by both JIT table builder and verify model builder, with explicit feature flags for runtime-only effects.

findings.summary: OData helpers leak two parallel metadata sources behind helper APIs
evidence: `resolve_entity_type`, `tenant_csdl_xml`, and `tenant_entity_sets` in `crates/temper-server/src/odata/common.rs` lines 62-98 each do registry-first then legacy fallback to `state.entity_set_map`/`state.csdl_xml`.
`crates/temper-server/src/observe/specs.rs` reads directly from `state.registry` only (lines 21-65, 75-119), so sibling read paths are not aligned on one abstraction boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single server metadata accessor (tenant-scoped) and migrate callers to it; keep legacy fallback in one compatibility adapter instead of per-helper branching.

findings.summary: Authz seam forces repetitive BTreeMap-to-HashMap translation
evidence: `authorize` and `authorize_with_context` in `crates/temper-server/src/state/entity_ops.rs` lines 205-243 and 253-290 both clone `BTreeMap` attrs into `HashMap` before calling authz.
`AuthzEngine::authorize` and `authorize_or_bypass` in `crates/temper-authz/src/engine.rs` lines 90-96 and 218-224 require `HashMap`, making each caller bridge collection types.
findings.suggestion: Define a single `ResourceAttrs` boundary type (or accept `impl IntoIterator<Item=(String, Value)>`) in authz engine so conversion happens once at the seam, not in each call path.

findings.summary: CLI serve startup packs many boundary responsibilities into one function
evidence: `crates/temper-cli/src/serve/mod.rs::run` (lines 60-260) handles storage backend creation, registry restoration, app loading, webhook aggregation, persistence wiring, hydration, and policy reload in one procedure.

Bootstrapping logic is separately encoded in `crates/temper-platform/src/bootstrap.rs` (`bootstrap_system_tenant`/`bootstrap_agent_specs`, lines 72-200), but serve startup still performs adjacent orchestration itself instead of composing clearer startup phases.
findings.suggestion: Split `run` into explicit startup phases (storage init, registry load, tenant bootstrap, integration wiring) and move each phase behind small typed components with clear input/output contracts.

findings.summary: Dispatch path has layered wrappers with low behavioral leverage
evidence: `crates/temper-server/src/state/dispatch/actions.rs` chains `dispatch_action` -> `dispatch_tenant_action` -> `dispatch_tenant_action_ext` -> `dispatch_tenant_action_core` (lines 16-178), with wrappers mainly forwarding arguments/defaults.

Callers like `create_system_entity` in `crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/operations.rs` lines 47-53 consume only the high-level entrypoint, while integration toggles are threaded through intermediate wrappers and options, increasing local call-path complexity.
findings.suggestion: Collapse wrapper levels into one public API plus one internal core, and encode optional behavior with explicit strategy methods instead of forwarding through multiple thin layers.

findings.summary: Similar evolution persistence loops diverge in error behavior
evidence: `persist_alerts` in `crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/operations.rs` lines 64-77 aborts with HTTP 500 on persistence failure, while `persist_insights` lines 117-129 only logs and continues; feature-request upserts (lines 231-244) also log-and-continue.

`crates/temper-platform/src/evolution/feedback.rs` uses yet another persistence style (`persist_evolution_record_to_pg`, lines 25-40, spawn-and-warn callsites at lines 106-112 and 143-149), creating inconsistent local failure semantics for the same domain concern.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one evolution persistence policy function that encodes severity (fail-fast vs best-effort) per record type, and route all server/platform evolution writes through it.

findings.summary: Multiple one-line passthrough modules add indirection without local behavior
evidence: `crates/temper-cli/src/util.rs` only re-exports naming helpers and defines no crate-specific behavior.
`crates/temper-mcp/src/repl.rs` only re-exports `temper_sandbox::repl::{ReplConfig, run_repl}`.
`crates/temper-jit/src/table/mod.rs` and `crates/temper-odata/src/query/mod.rs` both act primarily as extra re-export layers.
findings.suggestion: Collapse purely passthrough modules into their parent module namespace where possible, or add explicit local policy/translation responsibilities so each module boundary earns its maintenance cost.

findings.summary: Deprecated API is re-exported with suppression, leaving migration state implicit
evidence: `crates/temper-evolution/src/lib.rs:27` uses `#[allow(deprecated)] pub use store::RecordStore;`, which suppresses debt instead of constraining it.
`crates/temper-evolution/src/records.rs:232` exposes `migration_required`, indicating migration concerns are still first-class in domain records while deprecated surface remains public.
findings.suggestion: Replace blanket deprecation suppression with an explicit migration bridge: isolate deprecated exports behind a clearly named compatibility module and define a removal target/version in code comments plus tests that enforce no new callsites to deprecated APIs.

findings.summary: Error enums overuse generic String payloads, reducing local intent precision
evidence: `AuthzError` variants (`Denied`, `PolicyParse`, `Engine`, etc.) all carry raw `String` payloads instead of typed fields/context.
`McpError` variants (`Transport`, `InvalidParams`, `Tool`, `Runtime`, `Config`) are mostly `String` wrappers despite distinct categories.
`ObserveError` includes multiple string payload variants (`InvalidQuery`, `UnknownColumn`, `ProviderError`, `ConnectionError`) with no structured metadata for downstream handling.
findings.suggestion: Introduce small typed payload structs/enums for recurrent categories (for example parse location, backend/source, resource identifiers) and convert at boundaries; keep display strings derived from structured fields rather than as the primary data model.

findings.summary: Write-side management flows are housed under the observe namespace despite explicit read/write split intent.
evidence: `api.rs:1-5` documents `/observe` as read-only, but `api.rs:42-65` routes write operations (`/specs/load-*`, `/wasm/modules/*`, `/evolution/.../decide`) into `crate::observe::*` handlers.
`observe/evolution.rs:1,8-14` describes and exports decision/sentinel/unmet-intent operations, including mutation-oriented handlers, under the observe package.
findings.suggestion: Move mutation handlers out of `observe` into a write-oriented module tree (for example `management` or `api_mutations`) and keep `observe` exclusively read/query handlers; update router wiring to match this boundary.

findings.summary: `authz_helpers` is positioned as utility code but owns cross-subsystem governance workflows.
evidence: `authz_helpers.rs:42-143` creates pending decisions, persists them, dispatches governance entity creation, and persists trajectories; this is business orchestration rather than header/auth utility behavior.
`api.rs:23,127-140` calls this helper from policy-management endpoint flow, meaning endpoint behavior depends on hidden side-effect orchestration behind a helper-style module name.
findings.suggestion: Split `authz_helpers` into a thin auth context utility module and a dedicated domain service module (for example `governance/denial_workflow.rs`) so file placement matches responsibility and change boundaries.

findings.summary: Observe route files simultaneously own HTTP transport, source selection, and persistence query policy.
evidence: `observe/agents.rs:85-112,130-168` performs direct Turso querying, fallback behavior, and HTTP response shaping in the same module.
`observe/entities.rs:63-125` chooses between actor state and event store paths inside route code, combining integration policy with endpoint surface.
This contrasts with domain-focused module placement in `constraint_engine.rs:67-176`, where enforcement logic is isolated from transport concerns.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an `observe/query_service` layer to own source-selection and query logic, and keep route modules as thin transport adapters that map request/response only.

findings.summary: The observe crate’s root API exposes a concrete backend, weakening domain-first package boundaries.
evidence: `lib.rs:8-11` frames the crate around `ObservabilityStore` and provider adapters, but `lib.rs:21,31` publicly exposes both `clickhouse` module and `ClickHouseStore` at the crate root.

`clickhouse.rs:11-97` is fully concrete HTTP adapter logic, so re-exporting it at root pulls provider detail into the primary package surface instead of keeping backend integration as an optional edge.
findings.suggestion: Make provider implementations opt-in (feature-gated or sub-crate exports) and keep the default root surface trait/schema/error-centric to preserve architectural layering.

findings.summary: Evolution API contract says multi-backend, runtime exposes Turso-only reads with silent empty fallback
evidence: README advertises persistence as "Postgres or Turso/libSQL" and marks Evolution Engine as done (README.md:41, README.md:166, README.md:216, README.md:219).

Observe handlers hardcode Turso semantics ("single source of truth") and return HTTP 200 empty payload when backend is absent/fails (crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:23-24, :67-80, :88-89, :118-127).

CLI storage bootstrap still creates/migrates Postgres evolution records store (crates/temper-cli/src/serve/storage.rs:49-54), showing backend capability exists but is not reflected in observe contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical evolution-read contract at the server boundary (trait/service) that supports both Postgres and Turso, and make observe handlers depend on it; if a backend is intentionally unsupported, explicitly document and return a typed error instead of empty success.

findings.summary: Insight pipeline treats SubmitSpec success as resolution, bypassing stated verification/approval governance contract
evidence: README states every change is gated by verification cascade and presents OPADI chain integrity as core governance behavior (README.md:111, README.md:154, README.md:219).

Unmet-intent resolution is inferred from any successful SubmitSpec event by entity_type, with no check for verification outcome, decision status, or deployment success (crates/temper-server/src/insight_generator.rs:112-125, :250-252).

Records API exposes status fields as governance truth to consumers (crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:35-38), so premature 'resolved' inference creates top-level contract dishonesty.
findings.suggestion: Resolve unmet intents from verified governance artifacts (e.g., decision approved + verification passed + spec active) rather than SubmitSpec success heuristics, and centralize this lifecycle mapping in one domain component used by both insight generation and observe views.

findings.summary: CLI init scaffolds a generic Rust app, not the governance/spec-first workflow described as primary product contract
evidence: README positions the primary usage model as agent-driven spec submission via Temper APIs and verification cascade (README.md:74-102, README.md:111-119).

Init command creates a hello-world `main.rs`, a placeholder Cargo manifest, and generic next steps (`temper codegen`) instead of a runnable spec-first governance flow (crates/temper-cli/src/init/mod.rs:90-105).
Scaffolded structure can lead users toward historical Rust-app ownership instead of the documented domain ownership boundary (Temper-mediated operations).
findings.suggestion: Rework `temper init` into a contract-aligned bootstrap: generate minimal IOA + CSDL + policy stubs, include a submit/verify/apply command path, and remove or de-emphasize generic Rust-app scaffolding unless explicitly requested via a separate template mode.

findings.summary: Entity history fallback queries wrong persistence key shape, risking cross-tenant misses/leakage
evidence: `get_entity_history` uses tenant-scoped actor key (`{tenant}:{entity_type}:{entity_id}`) but event-store fallback reads `format!("{entity_type}:{entity_id}")` (`observe/entities.rs:64,101-102`).
Entity persistence/replay consistently use tenant-prefixed IDs (`entity_actor/actor.rs:104-107`) and store-loading does the same (`state/entity_ops.rs:435-436`).
findings.suggestion: Normalize persistence ID construction through a shared helper used by actor persistence, state hydration, and observe history endpoints; include tenant in fallback reads and add regression tests for same `entity_type/entity_id` across two tenants.

findings.summary: Evolution record serialization/parsing failures are silently converted into empty or partial data
evidence: Persistence path replaces serialization errors with defaults (`unwrap_or_default` / `"[]"`) before writing records (`operations.rs:63,89,116,223`).
Read path only merges record `data` when JSON parse succeeds and otherwise returns base metadata without surfacing corruption (`records_list.rs:40-47,98-105`).
findings.suggestion: Treat serialization/deserialization failure as explicit error paths: return/store a failure status with diagnostics instead of default JSON, and include a parse_error field in list responses when stored data is invalid.

findings.summary: Tenant resolution differs by endpoint, coupling behavior to bootstrap state and route choice
evidence: API endpoints default missing tenant header to literal `"default"` (`api.rs:1070-1073,1130-1133,1195-1199`; trajectories endpoint also defaults body tenant to `"default"` at `observe/evolution/trajectories.rs:96-99`).
OData helper falls back to first registered tenant in registry, then `TenantId::default()` (`odata/common.rs:23-36`), which can diverge from API behavior when startup tenant set/order changes.
findings.suggestion: Define one tenant resolution policy (header required, or deterministic configured default) in a single helper and reuse it across API/OData/observe handlers; add startup validation to ensure configured default tenant exists.

findings.summary: Webhook auth headers are bound to environment at startup and silently blank on missing vars
evidence: Webhook config expansion resolves `${ENV_VAR}` once in `from_toml` and substitutes unknown variables with empty string (`webhooks.rs:72-85,176-189`).
Dispatcher is created once during `temper serve` startup and reused (`serve/mod.rs:194-218`), so secret availability/rotation after bootstrap is not reflected.
findings.suggestion: Fail fast on unresolved required env placeholders and support explicit reload/re-resolution of webhook configs (or resolve per dispatch with cached TTL) to remove startup-order dependence for secrets.
